name: Pomegranate Jelly
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces Liquid pectin or 2 ounces Dry pectin
recipeInstructions:
1. To extract juice, cut crowns off the pomegranates and score the peel of each in several places.
2. Immerse the pomegranates one at a time in cool water in a large bowl; break into sections and separate seeds.
3. Skim off floating peel and membrane; discard. Drain seeds.
4. In a 5- to 6-quart kettle on high heat, combine seeds and 1/2 cup water. Cover and cook until seeds are soft when pressed, about 10 minutes.
5. Set colander lined with cheesecloth in a bowl. Pour in seeds and liquid. Tie cloth closed.
6. Wearing rubber gloves, squeeze bag to extract remaining juice. Measure; you need exactly 4 cups (if amount is short, add water).
7. If using liquid pectin, combine the pomegranate juice, lemon juice, and sugar in an 8- to 10-quart kettle; bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
8. Add liquid pectin and bring to a boil that cannot be stirred down. Boil, stirring, for exactly 1 minute.
9. If using dry pectin, combine the pomegranate juice, lemon juice, and pectin in an 8- to 10-quart kettle; bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
10. Stir in sugar and bring to a boil that cannot be stirred down; boil, stirring, exactly 2 minutes.
11. Pour hot jelly into within 1/4 inch of the rim of hot, sterilized, 1- or 2-cup canning jars. Wipe rims and put hot lids and rings in place.
12. Set jars on a rack in a deep kettle; add boiling water to cover.
13. Bring to simmering; simmer for 10 minutes. Cool jars on a towel for 2 days. Makes about 7 cups.
